summary: This week's AI news highlights significant developments in the industry, including Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI, advancements in AI-powered hardware, and growing concerns over AI safety and regulation. Key players like Google, NVIDIA, and Databricks are making strides in AI innovation, while policymakers are taking steps to address the technology's implications.
insight: The intersection of AI and human creativity is becoming increasingly prominent, with AI-generated content raising questions about authorship and ownership, and sparking debates over the role of AI in artistic expression.
highlights: Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI could disrupt the company's IPO plans and impact the broader AI industry.
Databricks has achieved a $188B valuation, solidifying its position as a leader in AI and data analytics.
Google is expanding its AI capabilities, including the introduction of personalized AI avatars and AI-powered game creation features.

articles.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
articles.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.

articles.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
articles.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

articles.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans

articles.title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
articles.description: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.

articles.title: What to watch for after Jensen Huang’s Japan visit
articles.description: Jensen Huang left Tokyo with deals spanning Japan's entire tech ecosystem.

articles.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
articles.description: Current AI, a non-profit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat and more.

articles.title: Fear of humanoid robots spurs human workers to strike at Hyundai auto factory
articles.description: Hyundai aims to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots starting with US factories in 2028.

articles.title: Linus Torvalds to critics of AI coding in Linux: "Fork it. Or just walk away."
articles.description: Creator says he will "very loudly ignore" those arguing for a ban on AI tools.
